title: Dependency-graph node (schema v2)
description: One node = one extracted primitive (module/package/class/function/variable). Extractors emit v2 primitives; classification adds the `classification` block. The v1 dossier-aware shape was retired in 2026-05 — `kind` is now the classification verdict, `primitive` is the structural category.

schema_version.description: Bump when the node format changes incompatibly.

id.description: Stable identity: <repo>::<repo-relative-path>::<symbol> for symbol primitives; <repo>::<repo-relative-path> for module/package; METHOD::/path for endpoints.

primitive.description: Structural primitive kind (set by the extractor — language-agnostic).

kind.description: Classification verdict (e.g. endpoint, component, hook, service, model, test, schema, module). Assigned by the plugin pipeline after extraction. Free-form so new plugins can introduce new kinds without a schema bump.

name.description: Symbol name as written (post-dot for methods).

owner.description: For methods: the containing class id. For functions with a dotted name, owner must be set. Null for top-level symbols.

signature.description: Callable surface — what hashing treats as identity-defining beyond name + body. Fields are optional so different primitive kinds use the relevant subset.

attributes.description: Boolean/scalar facts about the symbol that don't fit signature (external, abstract, generated, mutable, etc.).

edges_out.description: Outgoing edges authored by the extractor. The reverse index lives separately at nodes/_index/by_target.json — do not store dependents on a node file.

classification.description: Plugin pipeline output: which plugins voted, what kind they assigned, why.

extractor.description: Which extractor wrote this file (path@version). Used to know what to re-run on regen.

structural_hash.description: SHA-256 over the canonical signature + body shape. Changing this is the trigger that marks dossier text and dependents as 'needs review'.

dossier.description: Optional path to an LLM-curated companion markdown (relative to depgraph/). Set by dossier-* tooling; not emitted by extractors.

dossier_state.description: Persisted dossier-state stamp written by `depgraph regen` (#38 sub-item E). Set only when the regen pass detected hash drift (`stale`) — otherwise computed live from the dossier file by `_shared.dossier_state`. Downstream consumers (graphui, dossier-rank) prefer this field when present.

tests.description: Test nodes that exercise this one.

coverage_caveats.description: Enum-keyed list of known blind spots for this node — graph edges the current extractors cannot capture given the node's shape (SQLAlchemy ORM relationships, FastAPI Depends chain, Pydantic field refs, etc.). The framework registry (depgraph/lib/coverage_caveats.py) owns the enum-to-description mapping; the dossier renderer surfaces them so readers know where the graph is known to be blind. See #55.

warnings.description: Extractor-emitted warnings: ambiguous call sites, dynamic URLs, unresolved symbols.

kind.description: Edge kind in the v2 EdgeKind enum (calls, imports, instantiates, returns, etc.).

via.description: How the dependency is realized (function_call, import, render, hook_call, db_query, websocket, test_fixture, string_url).

confidence.description: Static-import edges are 'exact'. Concatenated/template URLs are 'fuzzy'. Edges added by hand or by an LLM are 'unresolved'.
